description: SEO/AEO/GEO content structure rules for all Pain System public assets.

principles: Answer-first: lead with the answer, not the context
Scannable: short paragraphs, numbered lists, clear headers
Action-oriented headings: use verb phrases, not noun phrases
No filler: cut any sentence that does not add information
Trust signals above fold: always show credibility markers early

FHI.primaryTarget: what to do if scammed UK
FHI.answerFirstRequirement: Direct answer within first 100 words of each guidance page
weaknesses: FHI homepage has no structured data markup
No FAQ section on homepage (missed AEO opportunity)
No breadcrumb navigation for fraud type sub-pages
Meta description could be more answer-forward
improvements: Add HowTo schema to action steps section
Add breadcrumb to fraud type pages
Optimise meta description: start with direct answer not brand name

VST.answerFirstRequirement: Clear value statement within hero — what it is, who it is for, why now
weaknesses: Hero may lead with product name rather than outcome
Compliance page needs AEO structuring for policy management queries
improvements: Rewrite hero to lead with outcome
Ensure pricing page CTA is above fold

geoRules.description: GEO = Generative Engine Optimisation — ensure content is AI-answer-ready
principles: Structured data makes content machine-readable for AI engines
Each section should be self-contained and quotable
Avoid ambiguous pronouns — name the subject explicitly
Include specific numbers, dates, and sources where possible
Each guidance item should have: what, why, how, when
